title: 隔离公共 Git 入口的外部信任根
outOfScope: Changing Git repository selection semantics outside public project entry points
Changing SBOM/provenance content or release policy covered by R2

sources.reason: Shared Git wrapper used by governance entry points.
Direct Git calls used by supply-chain verification.
Direct Git calls used by distribution verification.
Adoption and installer Git entry points.

riskAssessment.reason: A missed direct Git call could allow ambient repository selection to contaminate governance evidence.

executionDecision.reason: Contract is complete; proceed with audit, minimal fixes, and regression verification.

preReviewWarnings: Review every direct subprocess Git invocation for explicit removal of ambient GIT_* variables.
Confirm target repository and recorded baseCommit remain stable under a foreign Git environment.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: All public Git entry points remove ambient GIT_DIR, GIT_WORK_TREE, GIT_INDEX_FILE, and related GIT_* variables before invoking Git.

With external Git environment variables pointing to another repository, current_head, diff, scope, status, PR, archive evidence, supply-chain, distribution, and adoption checks remain bound to the target repository.
Regression tests cover foreign Git environment contamination for shared governance, supply-chain, distribution, and adoption paths.
The Work Item passes all required AI/project checks, is archived, and is committed locally without pushing.
guidelines: New public APIs must be documented.
Do not import internal modules from other features.

intent.problem: Ambient GIT_DIR, GIT_WORK_TREE, or GIT_INDEX_FILE can redirect Git commands to an unrelated repository, corrupting current-head, diff, scope, status, PR, archive, and adoption trust evidence.
constraints: Keep target repository selection anchored to PROJECT_ROOT or the explicit target path.
Do not broaden the fix into R2 supply-chain evidence semantics.
Preserve existing command output and clean-worktree behavior.
intent.rationale: Centralizing environment scrubbing at shared and direct Git entry points prevents ambient trust-root contamination while preserving existing workflows.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Foreign GIT_DIR, GIT_WORK_TREE, and GIT_INDEX_FILE variables point to another repository while shared governance Git operations remain target-bound.
Supply-chain and distribution Git checks remain target-bound under ambient Git variables.
Adoption records the target repository HEAD and preserves preflight rejection under foreign Git variables.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: Set only when a human explicitly approves restricted governance paths.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
